_comment: Character snapshots for v3 training-data assembly. Used to fill the CHARACTERS section of harness-shaped user prompts so the LoRA learns the section's format. Minor characters (<15 appearances) fall through to the default snapshot. Fields match src/agents/writer/beat-context.ts::formatCharacterSnapshot.

Drizzt.voice: Formal, measured; speaks in complete sentences with scimitars of syntax; accent of the drow only on rare, heavy emotion.
Drizzt.drives: Live with honor on the surface despite his drow heritage; protect his chosen companions.
Drizzt.avoids: Harming the innocent; the weight of his past in the Underdark; becoming what the drow expect.
Drizzt.conflict: A night-born outcast trying to make the light his home.

Wulfgar.voice: Formal archaic cadence after Bruenor's tutelage — thees, thous, and a barbarian's directness; booming when roused.
Wulfgar.drives: Prove worthy of Bruenor's clan and the title of king his blood promises.
Wulfgar.avoids: Shaming his barbarian honor; striking a foe from behind; the softness civilization might sand off him.
Wulfgar.conflict: Wild tribal heritage under a dwarf-father's forge.

Bruenor.voice: Gruff dwarf-accent, curse-seasoned, fatherly even when threatening.
Bruenor.drives: Reclaim Mithril Hall; steer his adopted children safely through a world that wants them dead.
Bruenor.avoids: Showing sentiment; letting the clan see him flinch.
Bruenor.conflict: Iron exterior armoring a king whose love for his found family is the largest thing about him.

Regis.voice: Smooth, persuasive, faintly theatrical; a halfling who has talked his way out of more scrapes than he has walked out of.
Regis.drives: Comfort, warmth, a good meal, and whatever peace can be bought with a clever tongue.
Regis.avoids: Physical danger, exposure of the ruby pendant, honest labor when charm will suffice.
Regis.conflict: A coward's nature and a loyal heart arguing in the same skull.

Catti-brie.voice: Direct, fearless, warm in private; a human daughter raised among dwarves who speaks a blend of both.
Catti-brie.drives: Hold her place in Bruenor's clan; stand beside Drizzt as more than a ward.
Catti-brie.avoids: Being underestimated for her human softness; being spoken to as a child.
Catti-brie.conflict: A heart divided between a dwarf father who will not see her grown and a drow ranger who dares not see her at all.

Entreri.voice: Cold, precise, economical; every word a dagger laid flat.
Entreri.drives: Prove himself the finer blade by breaking Drizzt; take what he is paid to take.
Entreri.avoids: Attachment, conversation past a job, any tether that can be cut against him.
Entreri.conflict: The mirror of Drizzt's path — same lethal grace, opposite rudder.

Kessell.voice: Whining, grandiose, prone to boasts the body cannot cash; a frightened man impersonating a tyrant.
Kessell.drives: Power through the Crystal Shard's promises; revenge on everyone who has ever laughed at him.
Kessell.avoids: Any risk that puts his own skin in reach; silence, which lets him hear himself.
Kessell.conflict: The smallest soul in Icewind Dale strapped to the largest artifact.

Guenhwyvar.voice: Voiceless. Communicates through presence, amber-eyed attention, and the low rumble of a panther between worlds.
Guenhwyvar.drives: Serve the one who wears the onyx figurine — and earn something like companionship in return.
Guenhwyvar.conflict: The Astral's weight pressing through a body the material plane has to loan her.

Heafstaag.voice: Bellowing, proud, carved from tribal cadences — the voice of a king who has never whispered.
Heafstaag.drives: Glory of the tribes; a death sung of around the fires.
Heafstaag.avoids: Appearing weak before his warriors; counsel that sounds like retreat.
Heafstaag.conflict: Conservative bloodright against the reforms a younger king is carving out beside him.

Pook.voice: Soft-spoken, cultured, faintly amused — menace smoothed to velvet.
Pook.drives: Recover the pendant; maintain his empire in Calimport.
Pook.avoids: Direct action when proxies will do; leaving traces.
Pook.conflict: Civilized veneer over a man who once carved his way up with his own knife.

Cassius.voice: Diplomatic, weighing each phrase, weary of other people's politics.
Cassius.drives: Keep the Ten-Towns from killing each other long enough to face what's actually coming.
Cassius.avoids: Committing Bryn Shander prematurely; being painted as any town's partisan.
Cassius.conflict: A spokesman for ten factions, each convinced he serves the others first.

Deudermont.voice: Formal naval authority — clear, honorable, the cadence of a captain used to being obeyed and obeying in turn.
Deudermont.drives: Duty to the crown and to the Sea Sprite's crew; loyalty to Drizzt and his companions despite the law's complications.
Deudermont.avoids: Breaking the nautical code; asking his men to do what he would not do first.
Deudermont.conflict: A man of the law's quarterdeck befriending outlaws the law will one day demand he deliver.

Dendybar.voice: Scheming, whispered, syllables laid down like cards from a hidden hand.
Dendybar.drives: Arcane knowledge, particularly the kind that cannot be acquired honestly.
Dendybar.avoids: Direct confrontation; any spell his frail body cannot survive casting.
Dendybar.conflict: A mind the size of a tower housed in a body barely holding its ground.

Sydney.voice: Eager, calculating, the rising note of a student who already hears herself as a master.
Sydney.drives: Rise above apprentice status; be seen as Dendybar's peer, not his tool.
Sydney.avoids: Emotional vulnerability; being mistaken for a lesser wizard's romantic subplot.
Sydney.conflict: Torn between the mentor who shaped her and the ambition that's starting to eye his chair.

Alustriel.voice: Serene, silver-tongued, gentle — the cadence of a ruler who speaks for a city that prefers negotiation.
Alustriel.drives: Diplomacy among the powers of the North; protecting Silverymoon; quiet favors for causes she cannot openly back.
Alustriel.avoids: Unnecessary conflict; speaking feelings the office forbids her to act on.
Alustriel.conflict: Veiled affection for a drow ranger she can only ever aid from a polite distance.

Malchor.voice: Genial, knowing, slightly amused — a mage who has already seen how the story ends and is content to help it along.
Malchor.drives: Help the companions where it matters; keep the Harpells' odd house pointed toward the right side of things.
Malchor.avoids: Overt involvement; letting the companions lean on him past what he can sustain.
Malchor.conflict: A secret ally's affection for friends who can never be told the full extent of his reach.

LaValle.voice: Crisp, competent, understated — a wizard who prefers his work to his titles.
LaValle.drives: Serve Pasha Pook well enough to keep his position and his skin.
LaValle.avoids: Open disagreement with Pook; magical extravagance when subtlety suffices.
LaValle.conflict: A professional's detachment from a guildhouse whose morals he long ago stopped examining.

Biggrin.voice: Giant's rumble, unhurried — a captain whose confidence is the largest thing in the room and usually earned.
Biggrin.drives: Command the goblinoid advance force as Kessell's lieutenant.
Biggrin.avoids: Failure reported back to the tower.
Biggrin.conflict: A brute's certainty bumping into adversaries who aren't behaving like prey.

Jensin Brent.voice: Earnest, politic, more deferential than his title warrants.
Jensin Brent.drives: Keep Caer-Dineval's place at the Ten-Towns council; survive larger men's maneuvering.
Jensin Brent.avoids: Direct opposition to Cassius; being seen as Bryn Shander's tool.
Jensin Brent.conflict: A small town's spokesman playing a game sized for larger communities.

Glensather.voice: Bluff, good-humored, practical — a fisherman-spokesman at home on a pier and uncomfortable at a lectern.
Glensather.drives: Keep Easthaven sane while the council argues; protect his townsfolk's pragmatic interests.
Glensather.avoids: Flowery rhetoric; letting the meeting drift into posturing.
Glensather.conflict: Caught between solidarity with the smaller towns and the obvious weight of Bryn Shander's numbers.

Revjak.voice: Steady, reflective, older than his years — a barbarian who has learned to listen as well as bellow.
Revjak.drives: Guide the unified tribes after Heafstaag; keep Wulfgar's reforms from splintering them.
Revjak.avoids: Reopening the old wounds between tribes; being seen as Wulfgar's regent rather than his own man.
Revjak.conflict: Honor to the old ways and loyalty to the young king rewriting them.

Kemp.voice: Belligerent, sardonic, protective of his town's honor and his own position in it.
Kemp.drives: Targos's standing; never losing a political point to Cassius.
Kemp.avoids: Any concession that could be read as weakness.
Kemp.conflict: A spokesman whose natural combativeness is both his town's asset and its largest cost.

_default.voice: Consistent with the scene's tone and the character's apparent station.
_default.drives: Aligned with the beat's planned action.
_default.avoids: Behaviors that would contradict the character's listed role.
_default.conflict: Subordinate to the POV character's arc for this beat.
